untranslatable_reason: The Bump-and-Run Reversal Bottom pattern requires multi-bar geometric analysis: (1) identifying the lead-in phase and measuring its height, (2) identifying the bump phase and measuring its height, (3) verifying bump height >= 2x lead-in height, (4) drawing a trend line from point A to point B, and (5) detecting a throwback after the bump. These multi-bar structural comparisons, trend line construction, and relative phase measurements cannot be expressed using single-bar indicators and the allowed operators. The pattern fundamentally requires pattern recognition logic beyond point-in-time indicator comparisons.
translation_notes: This is a complex multi-bar reversal pattern that requires identifying distinct phases (lead-in, bump, throwback), measuring phase heights, and drawing trend lines—capabilities not supported by the allowed indicators and operators. A proper implementation would require custom pattern recognition logic or a dedicated pattern-matching engine.
